title: thymus bulgaricus
intro: Thymus bulgaricus, also known as Bulgarian thyme, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family that is native to Southeastern Europe, particularly in the countries of Bulgaria and Greece. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Bulgarian thyme include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and boosting the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds, coughs, and sore throats. Bulgarian thyme can be prepared as a tea, essential oil, or added to foods and can also be used topically as a skin toner or in skincare products.
